content.content:
1→# Minimal workflow schema (example instance)
     2→version: v1alpha1
     3→id: my-workflow
     4→target_repo: org/project
     5→target_branch: refs/heads/main
     6→base_ref: refs/heads/main
     7→lane: C                 # optional global override (else auto-detect)
     8→build_timeout: 10m      # global build timeout
     9→self_heal:
    10→  max_retries: 2        # retries for ORW-based healing on build failure
    11→
    12→budgets:
    13→  run_max_tokens: 0      # 0 means unlimited
    14→  run_max_cost: 0        # 0 means unlimited
    15→  run_timeout: 0s        # 0s means unlimited
    16→
    17→artifacts:
    18→  type: mr               # full-archive | diff | mr
    19→  target_repo: org/project
    20→  storage:
    21→    diffs: seaweedfs
    22→    process_state: consul_kv
    23→
    24→mr:
    25→  forge: gitlab
    26→  repo_url_env: GITLAB_URL
    27→  token_env: GITLAB_TOKEN
    28→  labels: ["ploy", "tfl"]
    29→
    30→branch_naming:
    31→  workflow_branch: "workflow/${id}/${timestamp}"
    32→  exec_branch: "exec/${id}/${seq}"
    33→
    34→job_defaults:
    35→  retries: 3
    36→  timeout: 30m
    37→
    38→network:
    39→  default_policy: deny
    40→  allow_llm: true
    41→  allow_mcp_endpoints: true
    42→  additional_allowed_urls: []
    43→
    44→gates:
    45→  enabled: [static_analysis, vuln_scan, compile]
    46→  disabled: [deploy, tests]
    47→
    48→error_policy:
    49→  on_failure:
    50→    max_retries: 2
    51→    fallback: human-step   # halt | human-step
    52→
    53→merge:
    54→  strategy: merge
    55→  auto_format: true
    56→  recheck_gates_post_merge: true
    57→
    58→steps:
    59→  - type: recipe
    60→    id: openrewrite-updates
    61→    engine: openrewrite
    62→    recipes:
    63→      - com.acme.FixNulls
    64→      - com.acme.UpdateApi
    65→
    66→  - type: llm-plan
    67→    id: plan-remediations
    68→    parallel: true
    69→    max_parallel_execs: 10
    70→    mcp_tools:
    71→      - name: file-system
    72→        endpoint: mcp://fs
    73→      - name: search
    74→        endpoint: mcp://rg
    75→    context:
    76→      - "src/**/*.java"
    77→      - "https://docs.example.com/api/v2"
    78→    budgets:
    79→      max_tokens: 0
    80→      max_cost: 0
    81→      timeout: 0s
    82→
    83→  # Example explicit exec; in practice llm-plan emits exec steps
    84→  - type: llm-exec
    85→    id: apply-fix-1
    86→    model: my-model@v1        # Resolved via ployman model registry (CRUD)
    87→    prompts:
    88→      - "Refactor null checks to Optional"
    89→    mcp_tools:
    90→      - name: file-system
    91→        endpoint: mcp://fs
    92→    context:
    93→      - "src/**/*.java"
    94→    budgets:
    95→      max_tokens: 0
    96→      max_cost: 0
    97→      timeout: 0s
    98→
    99→  - type: human-step
   100→    reason: "Resolve merge conflicts if any"
   101→

content.content:
1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→// Job submission types for transflow healing workflow
     9→
    10→// JobSpec describes a job to be submitted to the orchestrator
    11→type JobSpec struct {
    12→	Name       string                 `json:"name"`
    13→	Type       string                 `json:"type"`
    14→	HCLPath    string                 `json:"hcl_path"`
    15→	EnvVars    map[string]string      `json:"env_vars"`
    16→	Timeout    time.Duration          `json:"timeout"`
    17→	Inputs     map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
    18→}
    19→
    20→// JobResult contains the result of a completed job
    21→type JobResult struct {
    22→	JobID     string                 `json:"job_id"`
    23→	Status    string                 `json:"status"` // "completed", "failed", "timeout"
    24→	Output    string                 `json:"output"`
    25→	Error     string                 `json:"error"`
    26→	Duration  time.Duration          `json:"duration"`
    27→	Artifacts map[string]string      `json:"artifacts"`
    28→}
    29→
    30→// PlanResult contains the output from a planner job
    31→type PlanResult struct {
    32→	PlanID  string                   `json:"plan_id"`
    33→	Options []map[string]interface{} `json:"options"`
    34→}
    35→
    36→// NextAction describes what the reducer determined should happen next
    37→type NextAction struct {
    38→	Action string `json:"action"`
    39→	Notes  string `json:"notes"`
    40→}
    41→
    42→// BranchSpec describes a healing branch to execute
    43→type BranchSpec struct {
    44→	ID     string                 `json:"id"`
    45→	Type   string                 `json:"type"`
    46→	Inputs map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// BranchResult contains the result of executing a healing branch
    50→type BranchResult struct {
    51→	ID         string        `json:"id"`
    52→	Status     string        `json:"status"`
    53→	JobID      string        `json:"job_id"`
    54→	Notes      string        `json:"notes"`
    55→	StartedAt  time.Time     `json:"started_at"`
    56→	FinishedAt time.Time     `json:"finished_at"`
    57→	Duration   time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    58→}
    59→
    60→// Interface definitions
    61→
    62→// JobSubmissionHelper provides methods for submitting transflow jobs
    63→type JobSubmissionHelper interface {
    64→	SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, config *TransflowConfig, buildError string, workspace string) (*PlanResult, error)
    65→	SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, planID string, results []BranchResult, winner *BranchResult, workspace string) (*NextAction, error)
    66→}
    67→
    68→// FanoutOrchestrator manages parallel execution of healing branches
    69→type FanoutOrchestrator interface {
    70→	RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error)
    71→}

1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"errors"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"time"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/api/arf"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// SelfHealConfig represents the self-healing configuration in transflow.yaml
    13→type SelfHealConfig struct {
    14→	MaxRetries int    `yaml:"max_retries"`
    15→	Cooldown   string `yaml:"cooldown"`
    16→	Enabled    bool   `yaml:"enabled"`
    17→}
    18→
    19→// Validate checks if the self-healing configuration is valid
    20→func (c *SelfHealConfig) Validate() error {
    21→	if c.MaxRetries < 0 {
    22→		return errors.New("max_retries cannot be negative")
    23→	}
    24→	if c.MaxRetries > 5 {
    25→		return errors.New("max_retries cannot exceed 5")
    26→	}
    27→	
    28→	if c.Cooldown != "" {
    29→		if _, err := time.ParseDuration(c.Cooldown); err != nil {
    30→			return fmt.Errorf("invalid cooldown format: %v", err)
    31→		}
    32→	}
    33→	
    34→	return nil
    35→}
    36→
    37→// ParseCooldown parses the cooldown duration
    38→func (c *SelfHealConfig) ParseCooldown() (time.Duration, error) {
    39→	if c.Cooldown == "" {
    40→		return 0, nil
    41→	}
    42→	return time.ParseDuration(c.Cooldown)
    43→}
    44→
    45→// GetDefaults returns a default self-healing configuration
    46→func GetDefaultSelfHealConfig() *SelfHealConfig {
    47→	return &SelfHealConfig{
    48→		MaxRetries: 1,
    49→		Cooldown:   "",
    50→		Enabled:    true,
    51→	}
    52→}
    53→
    54→// TransflowHealingAttempt represents a single healing attempt in the transflow context
    55→// This is simpler than the complex ARF HealingAttempt and focused on transflow needs
    56→type TransflowHealingAttempt struct {
    57→	AttemptNumber    int                `json:"attempt_number"`
    58→	ErrorContext     arf.ErrorContext   `json:"error_context"`
    59→	SuggestedRecipes []string           `json:"suggested_recipes"`
    60→	AppliedRecipes   []string           `json:"applied_recipes"`
    61→	Success          bool               `json:"success"`
    62→	ErrorMessage     string             `json:"error_message,omitempty"`
    63→	Duration         time.Duration      `json:"duration"`
    64→	Timestamp        time.Time          `json:"timestamp"`
    65→}
    66→
    67→// NewTransflowHealingAttempt creates a new healing attempt
    68→func NewTransflowHealingAttempt(attemptNumber int, errorContext arf.ErrorContext, suggestedRecipes []string) *TransflowHealingAttempt {
    69→	return &TransflowHealingAttempt{
    70→		AttemptNumber:    attemptNumber,
    71→		ErrorContext:     errorContext,
    72→		SuggestedRecipes: suggestedRecipes,
    73→		AppliedRecipes:   []string{},
    74→		Success:          false,
    75→		Timestamp:        time.Now(),
    76→	}
    77→}
    78→
    79→// MarkSuccess marks the healing attempt as successful
    80→func (a *TransflowHealingAttempt) MarkSuccess(appliedRecipes []string, duration time.Duration) {
    81→	a.Success = true
    82→	a.AppliedRecipes = appliedRecipes
    83→	a.Duration = duration
    84→	a.ErrorMessage = ""
    85→}
    86→
    87→// MarkFailure marks the healing attempt as failed
    88→func (a *TransflowHealingAttempt) MarkFailure(appliedRecipes []string, errorMsg string, duration time.Duration) {
    89→	a.Success = false
    90→	a.AppliedRecipes = appliedRecipes
    91→	a.ErrorMessage = errorMsg
    92→	a.Duration = duration
    93→}
    94→
    95→// TransflowHealingSummary tracks the overall healing process for a transflow run
    96→type TransflowHealingSummary struct {
    97→	Enabled       bool                         `json:"enabled"`
    98→	AttemptsCount int                          `json:"attempts_count"`
    99→	MaxRetries    int                          `json:"max_retries"`
   100→	Attempts      []*TransflowHealingAttempt   `json:"attempts"`
   101→	FinalSuccess  bool                         `json:"final_success"`
   102→	TotalHealed   int                          `json:"total_healed"`
   103→	TotalDuration time.Duration                `json:"total_duration"`
   104→	
   105→	// Job-based healing workflow fields
   106→	PlanID     string         `json:"plan_id,omitempty"`
   107→	Winner     *BranchResult  `json:"winner,omitempty"`
   108→	AllResults []BranchResult `json:"all_results,omitempty"`
   109→}
   110→
   111→// NewTransflowHealingSummary creates a new healing summary
   112→func NewTransflowHealingSummary(enabled bool, maxRetries int) *TransflowHealingSummary {
   113→	return &TransflowHealingSummary{
   114→		Enabled:       enabled,
   115→		AttemptsCount: 0,
   116→		MaxRetries:    maxRetries,
   117→		Attempts:      []*TransflowHealingAttempt{},
   118→		FinalSuccess:  false,
   119→		TotalHealed:   0,
   120→	}
   121→}
   122→
   123→// AddAttempt adds a healing attempt to the summary
   124→func (s *TransflowHealingSummary) AddAttempt(attempt *TransflowHealingAttempt) {
   125→	s.Attempts = append(s.Attempts, attempt)
   126→	s.AttemptsCount++
   127→	s.TotalDuration += attempt.Duration
   128→	
   129→	if attempt.Success {
   130→		s.TotalHealed++
   131→	}
   132→}
   133→
   134→// SetFinalResult sets the final success status
   135→func (s *TransflowHealingSummary) SetFinalResult(success bool) {
   136→	s.FinalSuccess = success
   137→}
   138→
   139→// HasReachedMaxRetries checks if max retries have been reached
   140→func (s *TransflowHealingSummary) HasReachedMaxRetries() bool {
   141→	return s.AttemptsCount >= s.MaxRetries
   142→}
   143→
   144→// BuildFailureAnalysis represents the analysis of build failure for recipe suggestions
   145→type BuildFailureAnalysis struct {
   146→	SuggestedRecipes []string  `json:"suggested_recipes"`
   147→	Confidence       float64   `json:"confidence"`
   148→	ErrorType        string    `json:"error_type"`
   149→	Language         string    `json:"language,omitempty"`
   150→	SourceFiles      []string  `json:"source_files,omitempty"`
   151→}
   152→
   153→// TransflowErrorAnalyzer analyzes build failures and suggests healing recipes
   154→type TransflowErrorAnalyzer struct {
   155→	// In the future, this might wrap the ARF analyzer
   156→	// For now, we'll use simple pattern matching
   157→}
   158→
   159→// NewTransflowErrorAnalyzer creates a new error analyzer for transflow
   160→func NewTransflowErrorAnalyzer() *TransflowErrorAnalyzer {
   161→	return &TransflowErrorAnalyzer{}
   162→}
   163→
   164→// AnalyzeBuildFailure analyzes build failure and suggests healing recipes
   165→func (a *TransflowErrorAnalyzer) AnalyzeBuildFailure(ctx context.Context, errors []string, language string) (*BuildFailureAnalysis, error) {
   166→	// For MVP, use simple error pattern matching to suggest common recipes
   167→	// In the future, integrate with the full ARF LLM analyzer
   168→	
   169→	errorContext := arf.ExtractErrorContext(errors, language)
   170→	
   171→	analysis := &BuildFailureAnalysis{
   172→		ErrorType:        errorContext.ErrorType,
   173→		Language:         language,
   174→		Confidence:       0.7, // Default confidence for pattern matching
   175→		SuggestedRecipes: []string{},
   176→	}
   177→	
   178→	// Simple pattern-based recipe suggestions
   179→	switch errorContext.ErrorType {
   180→	case "import":
   181→		analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes, "org.openrewrite.java.RemoveUnusedImports")
   182→		analysis.Confidence = 0.8
   183→		
   184→	case "compilation":
   185→		// Suggest common compilation fixes
   186→		if language == "java" {
   187→			analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes, 
   188→				"org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.SimplifyBooleanExpression",
   189→				"org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses")
   190→		}
   191→		analysis.Confidence = 0.6
   192→		
   193→	case "dependency":
   194→		analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes, "org.openrewrite.java.dependencies.DependencyVulnerabilityCheck")
   195→		analysis.Confidence = 0.5
   196→		
   197→	default:
   198→		// For unknown errors, suggest general cleanup recipes
   199→		if language == "java" {
   200→			analysis.SuggestedRecipes = append(analysis.SuggestedRecipes, 
   201→				"org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.CommonStaticAnalysis")
   202→		}
   203→		analysis.Confidence = 0.4
   204→	}
   205→	
   206→	// Extract source files if available
   207→	if errorContext.SourceFile != "" {
   208→		analysis.SourceFiles = []string{errorContext.SourceFile}
   209→	}
   210→	
   211→	return analysis, nil
   212→}

content.text: Let me also check what job templates and diff validation exist:

content.content:
1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"sync"
     7→	"time"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// fanoutOrchestrator implements the FanoutOrchestrator interface
    11→type fanoutOrchestrator struct {
    12→	submitter interface{} // MockJobSubmitter in tests, real submitter in production
    13→}
    14→
    15→// NewFanoutOrchestrator creates a new fanout orchestrator
    16→func NewFanoutOrchestrator(submitter interface{}) FanoutOrchestrator {
    17→	return &fanoutOrchestrator{
    18→		submitter: submitter,
    19→	}
    20→}
    21→
    22→// RunHealingFanout executes parallel healing branches with first-success-wins semantics
    23→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error) {
    24→	if len(branches) == 0 {
    25→		return BranchResult{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("no branches to execute")
    26→	}
    27→
    28→	// Create context for cancellation when first branch succeeds
    29→	fanoutCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
    30→	defer cancel()
    31→
    32→	// Channel to receive results
    33→	resultCh := make(chan BranchResult, len(branches))
    34→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
    35→
    36→	// Semaphore to limit parallelism
    37→	sem := make(chan struct{}, maxParallel)
    38→
    39→	// Launch all branches
    40→	for _, branch := range branches {
    41→		wg.Add(1)
    42→		go func(b BranchSpec) {
    43→			defer wg.Done()
    44→
    45→			// Acquire semaphore slot
    46→			select {
    47→			case sem <- struct{}{}:
    48→			case <-fanoutCtx.Done():
    49→				// Context cancelled, branch doesn't execute
    50→				resultCh <- BranchResult{
    51→					ID:     b.ID,
    52→					Status: "cancelled",
    53→					Notes:  "cancelled before execution",
    54→				}
    55→				return
    56→			}
    57→			defer func() { <-sem }() // Release semaphore slot
    58→
    59→			// Execute the branch
    60→			result := o.executeBranch(fanoutCtx, b)
    61→			resultCh <- result
    62→
    63→			// If this branch succeeded and context isn't cancelled, cancel others
    64→			if result.Status == "completed" {
    65→				cancel()
    66→			}
    67→		}(branch)
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	// Collect results
    71→	var allResults []BranchResult
    72→	var winner BranchResult
    73→	foundWinner := false
    74→
    75→	// Wait for all branches to complete or be cancelled
    76→	go func() {
    77→		wg.Wait()
    78→		close(resultCh)
    79→	}()
    80→
    81→	for result := range resultCh {
    82→		allResults = append(allResults, result)
    83→		
    84→		// First successful result becomes the winner
    85→		if result.Status == "completed" && !foundWinner {
    86→			winner = result
    87→			foundWinner = true
    88→		}
    89→	}
    90→
    91→	if !foundWinner {
    92→		return BranchResult{}, allResults, fmt.Errorf("all branches failed, no winner")
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	return winner, allResults, nil
    96→}
    97→
    98→// executeBranch executes a single branch and returns the result
    99→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) executeBranch(ctx context.Context, branch BranchSpec) BranchResult {
   100→	startTime := time.Now()
   101→	
   102→	result := BranchResult{
   103→		ID:        branch.ID,
   104→		Status:    "failed", // Default to failed
   105→		StartedAt: startTime,
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Check if context is already cancelled
   109→	select {
   110→	case <-ctx.Done():
   111→		result.Status = "cancelled"
   112→		result.Notes = "context cancelled"
   113→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   114→		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   115→		return result
   116→	default:
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	// Use type assertion to check if this is a test submitter
   120→	if testSubmitter, ok := o.submitter.(interface {
   121→		SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx context.Context, spec JobSpec) (JobResult, error)
   122→	}); ok {
   123→		spec := JobSpec{
   124→			Name:    branch.ID,
   125→			Type:    branch.Type,
   126→			Inputs:  branch.Inputs,
   127→			Timeout: 30 * time.Minute, // Default timeout
   128→		}
   129→
   130→		jobResult, err := testSubmitter.SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx, spec)
   131→		result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   132→		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   133→
   134→		if err != nil {
   135→			result.Status = "failed"
   136→			result.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("job execution failed: %v", err)
   137→		} else {
   138→			result.JobID = jobResult.JobID
   139→			result.Status = jobResult.Status
   140→			result.Notes = jobResult.Output
   141→		}
   142→
   143→		return result
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	// For production implementation, would use actual job submission
   147→	result.Status = "failed"
   148→	result.Notes = "production job submission not implemented yet"
   149→	result.FinishedAt = time.Now()
   150→	result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   151→	return result
   152→}

content.text: Now let me check a few existing tests to understand the testing patterns:
